message.content: {"decisionId": "full-e1-000-turn-1-player-0", "agentId": "plain-llm", "chosenActionId": "play-single-spade-3-copy1", "reasoning": "I hold only low spades and have the opening lead. I cannot see partner or opponent hands, so I avoid assuming their cards. Playing the smallest card preserves my higher spades for later tricks and makes a minimal opening lead."}

{"agentId":"plain-llm","decisionId":"full-e1-002-turn-1-player-0","chosenActionId":"play-single-diamond-3-copy1","reasoning":"I am leading the first trick with a non-trump 3-4-5-6 diamond sequence and no trumps or wildcards. My partner has only 2 cards left, so the priority is to help them go out. Playing the lowest card, the 3 of diamonds, gives my partner the easiest chance to beat the lead with a small non-trump or trump and continue toward finishing. A higher lead would make it harder for partner to win the trick and would increase the chance I win it myself, which is less valuable when partner is near finish."}
